name: Teacakes Grandma-Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and sugar until smooth.
2. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
3. Stir in the vanilla extract.
4. Gradually add the baking powder and salt to the mixture.
5. Mix in the flour until just combined.
6. Roll out the dough on a floured surface.
7. Cut into desired shapes using a knife or cookie cutter.
8. Place on an ungreased baking sheet.
9. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 8 to 10 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
When I was a teenager, I can remember my grandma telling me the story about the small town of Pickwick. Pickwick was done away with because the dam was going to be built. She was a native of Palo Pinto County. My grandpa, Lon L. Mason, grew up in Palo Pinto, Texas. She and I had many conversations about history while eating her Teacakes. Try the recipe, and maybe, you can remember a story or two to tell.
Shirley (Mason) Gibson for
Ida Mae (Hale) Mason, (deceased)
